items.title: e-Derma compulsory for new Subsection 44(6) applications from June 15 – IRB
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: All new applications for approval under Subsection 44(6) of the Income Tax Act 1967 involving institutions or organisations (IOs) must be submitted through the e-Derma system from June 15, 2026. According to IRB, the e-Derma system allows applications to be submitted online at any time without the need to visit an IRB office or submit physical documents. However, new applications under Subsection 44(6) involving funds or Institutions/Organisations/Funds (IOTs) represented by tax representatives or tax agents must continue to be submitted manually together with supporting documents for the time being.

items.title: Army tightens border control, records operation success almost daily
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The army has continued to tighten control along the country's borders to curb smuggling activities, recording successful arrests and seizures almost every day. Army Chief Gen Tan Sri Azhan Md Othman said security along the border had never been relaxed, with personnel remaining on high alert at all times to ensure the country's sovereignty is safeguarded. "All planned border control operations throughout the country have so far been carried out smoothly," he told reporters after officiating the Thoriq Bin Ziyad Mosque upgrading project and Forum Perdana Programme held in conjunction with the 93rd Army Day celebration at Kem Perdana Sungai Besi here today.

items.title: Galatek Technologies eyes US$100mil investment in Penang
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Galatek Technologies plans to invest up to US$100 million in Penang in five years following the opening of its first manufacturing, assembly and delivery facility in Malaysia. The facility represents Galatek's first manufacturing footprint in Malaysia and will serve as a key production and delivery centre supporting customers across Southeast Asia and international markets. Backed by investors including Granite Asia, Galatek invested US$2 million (RM9.4 million) in its initial Malaysian operations.

items.title: PN chairman Samsuri to attend 'Reset' movement convention tomorrow
items.summary: KOTA BARU: Perikatan Nasional chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar will attend the "Reset" Malaysia Convention tomorrow in Tanah Merah. Samsuri, who is also Pas vice-president, will join other Pas leaders at the event that will be officiated by Pas president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang. The event, where former Bersatu deputy president Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin is expected to unveil the movement's direction, will be held at the Kota Harmoni Expo Site.

items.title: Welfare Dept seeks family of girl under care since 2023
items.summary: BUKIT MERTAJAM: The Welfare Department is seeking the family of a six-year-old girl who has been under its care since March 2023. In a statement, the department said efforts to locate the child's biological mother, identified as Ida Rohana, an Indonesian, have so far been unsuccessful despite a media appeal issued on March 10, 2023. The department said it is issuing a second public appeal to trace the child's next of kin to facilitate welfare assessments and long-term planning for her future.

items.title: Unilateral child conversion laws in six states stand as High Court dismisses challenge by Indira Gandhi, 13 others
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 12 — The High Court today dismissed a challenge by Ipoh mother M. Indira Gandhi and 13 others against state laws that allow children under age 18 to be converted to Islam without both parents’ consent. Court of Appeal judge Datuk Aliza Sulaiman, who was a High Court judge when this case was heard, said that all 14 of them had failed to show they have locus standi, meaning the legal standing, to pursue this court challenge. But as this court challenge was filed as a public interest matter, the judge did not order Indira and the 13 others to pay for legal costs.

items.title: Genting Plantations unit unveils RM80bil Johor Tech Smart City
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Genting Plantations Bhd's subsidiary Genting Property Sdn Bhd has launched a large-scale development combining advanced agriculture, technology and smart infrastructure to support future-ready food systems and urban growth. Spanning 930.8 hectares, the Johor Tech Smart City, project is expected to generate more than RM80 billion in gross development value (GDV) and create over 10,000 jobs. Genting Property said the project will integrate agricultural technology (AgTech), artificial intelligence, sustainable infrastructure and smart urban planning into a single ecosystem.

items.title: Court dismisses Indira Gandhi's suit on unilateral conversion laws
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The High Court today dismissed a lawsuit by M. Indira Gandhi and 13 others seeking to invalidate Islamic laws in several states that allegedly allow the unilateral conversion of minors to Islam. The lawsuit, filed in March 2023, sought declarations that provisions in Islamic laws in Johor, Perlis, Melaka, Kedah, Negri Sembilan, Perak and the Federal Territories, which allegedly permit the unilateral conversion of minors to Islam, are unconstitutional and contrary to Article 12(4) of the Federal Constitution. The plaintiffs also sought declarations that unilateral conversions carried out after the Federal Court's landmark 2018 ruling in Indira Gandhi's case were invalid.

items.title: Three Indonesian children reunited with families after being found abandoned
items.summary: The exercise was successfully carried out through close cooperation among the department, the Indonesian consulate-general and the Immigration Department. Penang Social Development, Welfare and Non-Islamic Religious Affairs Committee chairman Lim Siew Khim said the successful reunification reflected the strength of cross-agency cooperation and bilateral ties between Malaysia and Indonesia in safeguarding children's welfare. Lim also thanked Indonesian consular officer Nelvy Meilia Syah, Penang Welfare Department director Rozita Ibrahim, the Immigration Department and the Welfare Department's Children Division personnel for their commitment and cooperation throughout the process.

items.title: Two small parties, one big decision: Hadi puts Gerakan and MIPP at centre of Perikatan power play
items.summary: MARANG, June 12 — PAS will decide on Bersatu’s future in Perikatan Nasional (PN) at its central working committee meeting next Wednesday, party president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang confirmed today, days after political cooperation between the coalition’s two biggest parties collapsed. Hadi said any expulsion from PN must be backed by a majority of the coalition’s four component parties: PAS, Bersatu, Gerakan and the Malaysian Indian People’s Party (MIPP). Hadi said PAS would leave it to both parties to choose between PAS and Bersatu in charting the coalition’s direction.

items.title: SD Property's Elmina Lakeside Mall wins global retail award
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Sime Darby Property Bhd's Elmina Lakeside Mall has won the World Gold Award under the 'Retail' category at the 2026 FIABCI World Prix d'Excellence Awards in Vienna, Austria. SD Property group managing director and chief executive officer Datuk Seri Azmir Merican said the recognition reflected the group's approach towards integrated township development. Built around retail, community and nature Located in the City of Elmina, Elmina Lakeside Mall was designed around the concept of "mall in a park, park in a mall", combining retail, leisure and community spaces within a walkable environment.

items.title: [UPDATED] MIPP picks Pas, snubs BN in polls alliance gamble
items.summary: MIPP president S. Punitha said the decision followed a meeting with Pas vice-president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar in Kuala Lumpur yesterday, where both sides discussed future political cooperation and the party's role within Perikatan Nasional (PN). Speaking in an interview with the New Straits Times, Punitha said Pas had offered MIPP a platform to strengthen Indian representation while respecting the distinct identities of both parties. Punitha said discussions also covered seat allocations for future elections, with MIPP expected to contest four Johor seats under the arrangement.

items.title: Police arrest 12, including 10 Chinese nationals, in Melaka scam call centre bust
items.summary: MELAKA, June 12 — The police dismantled a ‘call centre’ syndicate suspected of being involved in a non-existent online investment scam with the arrest of 12 individuals in two raids at Taman Krubong Perdana here early on Wednesday morning. Acting Melaka Tengah district police chief Supt Halim Abas said that those detained comprised 10 male Chinese nationals, in addition to a local man and woman, all aged between 23 and 47 years. “In the first raid at 2.30 am, a team from the Melaka Commercial Crime Investigation Department (JSJK) arrested six individuals, consisting of five male Chinese nationals and one local man, all aged between 23 and 43 years.

items.title: Vietnam police bust group planning large-scale online scam centre
items.summary: HANOI: Vietnamese police have busted a group suspected of trying to establish a large-scale online scam centre in the country, authorities said on Friday, as criminal networks spread their operations across Southeast Asia. Police in Phu Tho province uncovered and disrupted a transnational group linked to online fraud syndicates operating in Cambodia, preventing it from setting up what they described as a major scam hub in Vietnam, the Ministry of Public Security said in a statement. The raid "prevented the formation of a large-scale transnational high-tech fraud centre within Vietnam," and also helped safeguard national security and protect people's assets, the ministry said.

items.title: Kedah schoolgirl unable to sit or stand since classmate’s chair-pulling prank in February
items.summary: PADANG BESAR, June 12 — A 12-year-old girl has allegedly been unable to attend school for nearly four months after sustaining injuries in a fall when the chair she was about to sit on was allegedly pulled away by a classmate during a lesson in February. Her mother, Norasikin Hasin, 32, claimed that the Year Six pupil suffered a soft tissue injury to her lower back and was subsequently diagnosed with functional neurological disorder and post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD) following the incident on Feb 11. She said the incident occurred during a teaching and learning session when her daughter attempted to sit down after greeting a teacher, but fell to the floor after her chair was allegedly pulled away by a female classmate.

items.title: Small portions, big problems — the incredible shrinking menu hits Malaysia
items.summary: According to the latest report from Retail Group Malaysia (RGM), sales at cafes and restaurants plummeted by 4.6 per cent in the first quarter of 2026 compared to the same period last year. This is a jarring reversal from the 3.1 per cent growth seen in the final quarter of 2025. Even more telling is the gap between hope and reality: as recently as March, F&B businesses had forecast a growth of 1.9 per cent for the quarter.

items.title: Cop acquitted and discharged of RM41,000 bribery charges
items.summary: TAWAU: A policeman was today acquitted and discharged by the Sessions Court here of three charges of soliciting and accepting RM41,000 in bribes. Inspector Saharulzaman Alizaman, 42, was cleared of all three charges at the close of the prosecution's case. Judge Ahmad Faizhad Yahaya, in delivering his ruling, said the prosecution's evidence was insufficient to support allegations that the accused had solicited and received money for corrupt purposes.

items.title: Obey Malaysian regulations or action will be taken, Anwar tells Rohingya refugees
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 12 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has reminded Rohingya refugees in the country to comply with local laws and regulations at all times or face firm action. He said that although Malaysia adopts a humanitarian approach, the group remains subject to the country's legal system, including regulations governing the use of premises and the operation of businesses. “My advice to Myanmar nationals, particularly the Rohingya, is that they must also comply with the regulations in our country.

items.title: Forestry authorities detect 21 firms for illegal logging in protected forests
items.summary: Peninsular Malaysia Forestry Department director-general Datuk Zahari Ibrahim said 17 companies were identified in 2025, while four more were detected between January and March this year. He said investigations and field analyses showed most large-scale forest reserve encroachment cases, particularly illegal logging, involved organised syndicates rather than individuals. Zahari said enforcement capabilities had been strengthened over the past two years through integrated monitoring systems capable of detecting encroachment and illegal logging in real time.

items.title: Second day of firefighting as dump blaze rages near IIUM
items.summary: Selangor Fire and Rescue Department operations assistant director Ahmad Mukhlis Mukhtar said the fire, which involved a rubbish heap, was still actively burning despite continuous firefighting operations since yesterday. The department received an emergency call on the incident at 10.59am yesterday and dispatched a Fire Rescue Tender (FRT) and an Emergency Medical Rescue Services (EMRS) vehicle from the Selayang Fire and Rescue Station. The first fire engine arrived at the scene at 11.12am, with seven firefighters led by operations commander PBK I Suhaimi Samoori deployed to contain the blaze.

items.title: Suspect in Kampung Gajah murder and rape case arrested in Perlis
items.summary: IPOH: A man suspected of fatally stabbing another man and raping his ex-girlfriend at Km12, Jalan Trans Perak, near Kampung Gajah, was arrested in Perlis. Perak police chief Datuk Mohd Alwi Zainal Abidin said the 30-year-old was detained at Perlis Sentral Bus Terminal in Kangar, about 3.45pm by a team from the Perak and Perlis Criminal Investigation Departments. Alwi said the boyfriend chased the suspect and managed to ram and block the man's vehicle.

items.title: Govt to take action against Rohingya refugees violating laws, says PM
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The government will take action against Rohingya refugees found violating the country's laws and regulations, including matters involving businesses and the construction of buildings. Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the issue of the increasing Rohingya population in Malaysia was discussed with Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail during today's Cabinet meeting. He said the Myanmar government was currently preventing the return of Rohingya refugees to the country.

items.title: Thailand declares 15-day national mourning for Princess Bajrakitiyabha
items.summary: BANGKOK: Thailand on Friday declared a 15-day period of national mourning following the death of Princess Bajrakitiyabha Narendira Debyavati, the eldest child of King Maha Vajiralongkorn, with flags to be flown at half-mast at government institutions nationwide. Government Spokesperson Ratchada Thanadirek said the national mourning arrangements were endorsed at a special Cabinet meeting after ministers were informed of the Princess's death by the Bureau of the Royal Household. She said the national mourning period took effect immediately, with Anutin directing all relevant agencies to ensure the royal funeral ceremonies are conducted with full honours and in accordance with royal traditions.

items.title: Oil extends losses as Trump calls off planned strikes on Iran
items.summary: SINGAPORE: Oil prices fell more than two per cent on Friday, extending losses from the previous session after US President Donald Trump cancelled plans to strike Iran, reducing fears of an escalation of hostilities following tit-for-tat attacks earlier in the week. Brent futures were down US$2.11 or 2.3 per cent at US$88.27 a barrel by 0640 GMT, while US West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude dropped US$1.90, or 2.2 per cent to US$85.81. The US military said on social media that commercial ships continued to transit the waterway.

items.title: Fire partially destroys Gebeng waste plant, 200 students evacuated
items.summary: KUANTAN: About 200 students from the Gebeng Advanced Technology Training Centre hostel were evacuated after a fire partially destroyed a waste management plant next to the hostel in the Gebeng industrial area today. About 100 firefighters, including volunteers from the Gebeng Emergency Mutual Aid (Gema) unit and nearby factories, battled the blaze for almost two hours. Pahang Fire and Rescue Department director Datuk Mohd Razam Taja Rahim said the department received a distress call at 6.29am, and firefighters from the Gebeng fire station arrived within 10 minutes.

items.title: Malaysia leads in Islamic derivatives, but adoption lags
items.summary: However, the rating agency said conventional derivatives still dominate the market, and it estimates only one per cent of derivatives were Islamic in 2025. Fitch said the majority of rated Islamic banks have deployed Islamic derivatives, but adoption is rare amongst rated Islamic insurance companies. However, it said the Islamic derivatives market still lags conventional derivatives in most core Islamic finance markets.

items.title: Rohingya labourer claims trial to robbing badminton legend Razif Sidek at knifepoint
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A Rohingya labourer pleaded not guilty at the Sessions Court today to robbing national badminton legend Datuk Mohamad Razif Mohd Sidek at knifepoint of RM60,000 worth of belongings last month. The accused entered his plea before Sessions Court judge Siti Shakirah Mohtaruddin. She informed the court that the accused's United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR) card had expired.

items.title: Global tech giants Agibot and Huawei enter Johor
items.summary: KULAI: Johor has officially positioned itself as a premier regional force in high-tech investment, securing partnerships with global technology titans, including Huawei and Agibot, to anchor the landmark RM80 billion Johor Tech Smart City project. "This initiative focuses on sectors including technology, artificial intelligence (AI), smart agriculture, and talent research and development (R&D). Johor Tech Smart City will be developed as an integrated ecosystem combining an AgTech Campus and a Knowledge AI Campus.

items.title: Why Johor votes first: Election Commission cites Negeri Sembilan’s surprise dissolution
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 12 — Logistical demands and preparations for election workers were among the main reasons Johor and Negeri Sembilan will not hold their state elections simultaneously, the Election Commission (EC) said today. “(The Negeri Sembilan assembly) was dissolved suddenly. “Negeri Sembilan only has the ballot boxes.

items.title: A year after deadly Air India crash, families await answers
items.summary: AHMEDABAD: Families of those killed in last year's Air India plane crash gathered at the site on Friday to mark the anniversary of the disaster, still awaiting answers about its cause. On June 12, 2025, a Boeing 787 crashed into a medical college shortly after take-off in India's western city of Ahmedabad, killing 260 people in the deadliest air disaster in a decade. Indian authorities are expected to issue an interim report in the coming days, a source of frustration for the victims' relatives, who had been expecting a final disclosure.

items.title: Motorcyclist who slashed rider with meat cleaver remanded for four days
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: A motorcyclist who allegedly attacked another rider with a meat cleaver on the Federal Highway motorcycle lane yesterday has been remanded for four days. District police chief Assistant Commissioner Sarudin Samah said the 43-year-old man was detained for investigations under Section 324 of the Penal Code for voluntarily causing hurt with a dangerous weapon. It was reported yesterday that a motorcyclist was injured after being slashed by another rider on the Federal Highway motorcycle lane near Section 13 early yesterday morning.

items.title: Labourer charged with robbing Razif Sidek at knifepoint in TTDI home, faces 14-year jail term and whipping
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 12 — A Rohingya labourer pleaded not guilty in the Sessions Court here today to robbing national badminton legend Datuk Razif Sidek at knifepoint at the latter’s home last month. Mohamad Fawyas Yasin, 27, was accused of robbing Datuk Mohamad Razif Mohd Sidek, 64, at a house in Jalan Damansara, Taman Tun Dr Ismail, at about 12.20pm on May 19. Deputy public prosecutor J. Banusha objected to bail, citing the seriousness of the offence and the accused’s expired United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR) card.

items.title: Pas to decide Bersatu's status in PN this Wednesday
items.summary: MARANG: Pas will decide on Bersatu's position within Perikatan Nasional at the party's central working committee meeting this Wednesday, Pas president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang said the removal of any party from PN depended on the majority decision of the coalition's component members. He said PN comprised four component parties, namely Pas as the largest party in the coalition, Bersatu, Gerakan and the Malaysian Indian People's Party. Hadi said Pas held the PN chairmanship and Bersatu was still a member, alongside Gerakan and MIPP.

items.title: Malaysia's factory output posts fastest growth since September 2022
items.summary: The mining sector returned to positive territory, expanding 6.8 per cent after contracting 6.5 per cent in March, while electricity generation increased 10.5 per cent, accelerating from 4.8 per cent previously. Electricity output, however, rose 2.1 per cent during the month. Month-on-month, domestic-oriented industries increased 5.3 per cent, improving from 1.4 per cent growth in March.

items.title: Education Ministry to probe alleged Rohingya community school in Terengganu
items.summary: Deputy Education Minister Wong Kah Woh said the Education Ministry will investigate claims of a Rohingya community school operating in Kampung Ladang Titian, Kuala Terengganu, before taking further action. "All schools are subject to the Education Act, and absolutely none can be established without approval. Wong was responding to media reports yesterday that a suspected Rohingya community school had sparked concern and unease among Kuala Terengganu residents since February.

items.title: Malaysians advised to avoid Central Jakarta demonstration areas
items.summary: JAKARTA: The Malaysian Embassy here has advised Malaysians in the capital to avoid demonstration hotspots in Central Jakarta today and closely follow updates from local authorities. In an advisory today, the embassy said it was monitoring reports of demonstrations, particularly around Bundaran HI and the Arjuna Wiwaha Statue, also known as Patung Kuda, where traffic disruptions were expected throughout the day. The embassy said the protests would cause traffic disruptions in several parts of Central Jakarta, particularly along Jalan MH Thamrin, Jalan Jenderal Sudirman and Jalan Medan Merdeka Barat.

items.title: A fatal crash, a TikTok livestream and a ‘pistol’: How sharp-eyed viewers helped Kedah police arrest two men
items.summary: SUNGAI PETANI, June 12 — A TikTok livestream of a fatal crash took a dramatic turn yesterday when eagle-eyed viewers spotted what appeared to be a pistol inside the wreckage – a discovery that led police straight to two men now in custody. “Both suspects will be remanded this morning, and the case is being investigated under Section 36 of the Arms Act 1960, Section 41(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987 and Section 44(1)(a) of the same Act,” he said. Section 44(1)(a) of the Road Transport Act 1987 governs driving under the influence of drugs or alcohol causing death.

items.title: KL police to double presence in 122 schools to combat illegal racing, traffic offences
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police will double their presence in 122 secondary schools here under a new traffic safety advocacy programme aimed at educating students on road safety and illegal street racing. Kuala Lumpur police chief Datuk Fadil Marsus said the Traffic Awareness Advocacy Programme, which began today, would also involve other police departments to strengthen crime prevention awareness among students. Also present were Federal Territory Education Department Planning and Management Sector deputy director Aziz Hashim, Kuala Lumpur Crime Prevention and Community Safety Department chief Senior Assistant Commissioner Ravinder Singh, and Kuala Lumpur Traffic Enforcement and Investigation Department chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Zamzuri Mohd Isa.

items.title: Kuala Lumpur police launch probe into station chief over corruption claims [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police have opened an investigation into allegations of corruption and abuse of power involving a station chief under the Wangsa Maju district police headquarters. Kuala Lumpur police chief Datuk Fadil Marsus said an investigation paper had been initiated to determine whether the allegations were substantiated or baseless. He said the district police chief and the Integrity and Standards Compliance Department had been instructed to carry out the investigation.

items.title: TikToker remanded four days over online claims involving rescued hiker Jaslinda
items.summary: TAPAH, June 12 — A man in his 30s has been remanded for four days from today to assist investigations into a defamation case involving an Orang Asli village head (Tok Batin) of Kampung Lubuk Gaharu during a TikTok live stream. The case is being investigated under Section 500 of the Penal Code, Section 233 of the Communications and Multimedia Act 1998, and Section 14 of the Minor Offences Act 1955. He was arrested at his home in Ipoh at about 1 pm yesterday following 11 police reports, Tapah district police chief Supt Abdul Malik Hasim said.

items.title: Malaysian embassy warns citizens to steer clear of Jakarta demonstrations
items.summary: JAKARTA, June 12 — The Embassy of Malaysia in Jakarta has advised Malaysians in Indonesia’s capital to avoid demonstration hotspots in Central Jakarta on Friday and follow updates from local authorities. In an advisory on Friday, the embassy said it was closely monitoring reports of demonstrations, particularly around Bundaran HI and the Arjuna Wiwaha Statue, also known as Patung Kuda, where traffic disruptions were expected throughout the day. The embassy said the protests were expected to cause traffic disruptions in several parts of Central Jakarta, particularly along Jalan MH Thamrin, Jalan Jenderal Sudirman and Jalan Medan Merdeka Barat.

items.title: Anwar to visit Sabah tomorrow for Madani Link launch and National Unity Week event
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, June 12 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim is scheduled to make a one-day visit to Sabah tomorrow to attend several events, including an official launch and community programmes. Upon completing the programme, he is scheduled to return to the state capital to attend the Sabah-level Melangkah Bersama Madani: Wanita Beriman, Wanita Bertahan event at the Sabah International Convention Centre at 2.30 pm. Anwar is scheduled to officiate the 2026 National Unity Week celebration at 4 pm.

items.title: EC sets Johor election on July 11, Negeri Sembilan on Aug 1
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 12 — The Election Commission (EC) today announced the polling dates for the upcoming state elections in Johor and Negeri Sembilan, setting the stage for a high-stakes political showdown in the southern region. EC chairman Datuk Seri Ramlan Harun confirmed that Johor voters will head to the polls on July 11, with nomination day scheduled for June 27 and early voting taking place on July 7. “For the 16th Negeri Sembilan elections, there will be 889,490 voters," the EC chairman added.

items.title: Ex-boyfriend held over rape allegation of 25-year-old woman, fatal stabbing of love rival in Perak
items.summary: Perak police chief Commissioner Datuk Mohd Alwi Zainal Abidin said the suspect was detained at the Perlis Sentral Bus Terminal in Kangar at about 3.45pm yesterday following intelligence leads, The Star r eported today. Investigators believe the suspect had raped his ex-girlfriend, 25, after they met near a paddy field along Jalan Trans Perak here on June 9. A confrontation ensued after the 29-year-old allegedly rammed the suspect’s vehicle during a car chase along Jalan Trans Perak.

items.title: LHDN makes e-Derma mandatory for new tax exemption applications from June 15
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 12 — All new applications for approval under Subsection 44(6) of the Income Tax Act 1967 involving institutions or organisations (IOs) must be submitted through the e-Derma system from June 15, 2026. According to LHDN, the e-Derma system allows applications to be submitted online at any time without the need to visit an LHDN office or submit physical documents. However, new applications under Subsection 44(6) involving funds or Institutions/Organisations/Funds (IOTs) represented by tax representatives or tax agents must continue to be submitted manually together with supporting documents for the time being.

items.title: Early morning fire hits Gebeng waste plant, smoke visible across Kuantan (VIDEO)
items.summary: KUANTAN, June 12 — Thick black smoke billowed across the Gebeng industrial area here after a fire broke out at a scheduled waste processing facility early today, prompting the temporary evacuation of about 200 students from a nearby training institute. National newswire Bernama reported Pahang Fire and Rescue Department director Datuk Mohd Razam Taja Rahim as saying that firefighters found the blaze had erupted inside the factory and involved oil-based materials and solvents. “The fire also affected the warehouse at the factory.

items.title: Miri main water pipeline repaired, supply to be return in stages within 12 hours
items.summary: MIRI, June 12 — Repair works on the damaged 1,100mm main pipeline at Mile 7 of Jalan Miri-Bintulu were completed at 8.30am, with water supply expected to be restored to affected areas in stages over the next 12 hours. Deputy Minister for Utility and Telecommunication Datuk Liwan Lagang said Sarawak Water is refilling the system, stabilising the distribution network, and carrying out flushing activities to ensure water quality is satisfactory before supply is fully restored to consumers. Among them were the Rural Water Supply Department, Fire and Rescue Department, Public Works Department, Sarawak Energy, Miri Resident’s Office, Miri District Office, Miri District Education Office, Miri City Council, Royal Malaysia Police, Malaysian Armed Forces, Malaysian Red Crescent, Ministry of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living, Miri Hospital, elected representatives, media personnel, and volunteers.

items.title: Umno sec-gen welcomes 21,858 new and returning members to ‘Rumah Bangsa’
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 12 — Umno has recorded 21,858 new and returning members within less than two months since online registration was opened on April 20, party secretary-general Datuk Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki said today. He said the figure covers new members as well as former leaders and members who have rejoined the party nationwide, including some from Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia (Bersatu). “Since online registration was officially opened on April 20, 2026 until the Umno supreme council meeting on June 10, 2026, in less than two months, the number of new registrations and returning former leaders and members of Umno, including leaders from Bersatu across Malaysia, totalled 21,858,” he said in a Facebook post.

items.title: Scam ‘salary’ of RM85 costs 37-year-old nearly RM100,000 in online job ruse
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU, June 12 — A 37-year-old housewife lost RM95,715 after falling victim to an online job scam that promised easy earnings but delivered only a token RM85 commission, Harian Metro reported today. Kuala Terengganu police chief Assistant Commissioner Azli Mohd Noor said the victim was first drawn in after seeing a job advertisement on Facebook on May 8. Azli said the victim only received RM85 for the first two tasks before the scam escalated into larger transfers.

items.title: Petaling Jaya MP Lee Chean Chung quits PKR leadership, says party ‘no longer’ what it was
items.summary: Lee, however, said he will remain a PKR member and continue serving as an MP until Parliament is dissolved. “PKR today is no longer the PKR I once knew,” he said in a Facebook post today announcing his decision. Lee also said he would continue to carry out his parliamentary role, including supporting institutional reform bills expected to be tabled in June.

items.title: Umno Youth chief’s challenge to prosecution over Facebook post set for June 16 hearing
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 12 — Umno Youth chief Datuk Dr Muhamad Akmal Saleh has filed a judicial review application challenging the Attorney General’s decision to prosecute him over a Facebook post allegedly capable of causing public fear. The application was filed at the High Court Penang on February 27 and is scheduled for hearing on June 16, according to his lawyers Messrs Aizat Izuan Shakeel, Harian Metro reported yesterday. In a statement, the firm said the case goes beyond Akmal’s personal circumstances and raises broader constitutional and systemic issues.

items.title: Bersama urges Selangor to revoke non-Muslim worship guidelines, restart consultations
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 12 — Parti Bersama Malaysia (Bersama) today urged the Selangor government to withdraw newly approved guidelines governing non-Muslim houses of worship and restart consultations with religious groups and other stakeholders. The party, led by former ministers Rafizi Ramli and Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad, also expressed solidarity with Petaling Jaya MP Lee Chean Chung, who recently raised concerns over the guidelines. The party said the guidelines, approved by the Selangor executive council last November, included restrictions on establishing non-Muslim houses of worship in commercial areas, limits on building heights, conditions restricting access to main roads, and a requirement of one house of worship for every 5,000 residents.

items.title: After eight years, Lebanese jeweller says ‘vindicated’ by RM67.5m judgment against Rosmah
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 12 — Lebanese jeweller Samer Halimeh today said he felt vindicated after winning an eight-year legal battle against Datin Seri Rosmah Mansor, whom the High Court ordered to pay RM67.5 million over 43 missing pieces of luxury jewellery. Last Tuesday, the High Court ordered Rosmah to pay RM67,461,027.37 within a month after finding her liable for failing to return 43 pieces of jewellery supplied on a consignment basis. The dispute centred on 44 pieces of luxury jewellery worth US$14.57 million that Global Royalty said were delivered to Rosmah in 2018 for viewing under a consignment arrangement.

items.title: DOE probes viral orange smoke incident in Pasir Gudang industrial area
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 12 — The Department of Environment (DOE) acted swiftly to investigate following a viral report on social media regarding the release of orange smoke, believed to have occurred in the Jalan Keluli area of Pasir Gudang yesterday morning. “However, based on information obtained on-site, the emission is believed to have originated from an industrial premises in the Pasir Gudang Industrial Area. As a precautionary measure, the Johor State DOE also conducted air quality monitoring at two identified sensitive locations, namely Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan (SMK) Taman Pasir Putih and Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan (SMK) Pasir Gudang 3.

items.title: Mass fish deaths in Sungai Padang Kijang trigger DOE probe
items.summary: The state DOE deputy director Mohamad Zamzani Ibrahim said the department dispatched two officers to the location immediately after receiving the complaints to assess the situation and collect river water samples for analysis. “Preliminary inspections were conducted using YSI ProDSS equipment to obtain in-situ water quality parameter readings as an initial assessment of the river’s condition,” he told Bernama yesterday. — Bernama

items.title: Will LIKE choke Brickfields? Residents, religious leaders seek answers on RM2.3b proposed highway
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 12 — The Brickfields community has raised the alarm over the proposed RM2.32 billion Laluan Istana-Kiara Expressway (LIKE), warning that the massive project risks "paralysing" the district’s already congested inner roads. Leaders from local religious institutions and the Malaysian Association for the Blind (MAB) met with project consultants Perunding Tamadun Teras Sdn Bhd yesterday, demanding transparency on how the 7.7km elevated highway will impact daily life and pedestrian safety, The Star r eported today. Even if the highway is elevated, the impact on traffic along the inner roads must be taken into consideration,” Sri Maha Muneswarar Temple representative A. Tanuja was quoted as saying, expressing concern over the unique nature of the township.

items.title: Anwar vows no compromise in anti-corruption fight, backs stronger MACC
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 12 — The MADANI Government will continue to empower the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) as a credible and respected institution in efforts to eradicate corruption without compromise, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said. The Prime Minister emphasised that efforts to strengthen governance, integrity, and the country’s institutional reform agenda form the core of building a fairer, more transparent, and competitive Malaysia. “The MADANI Government will continue to empower the MACC as a credible and respected institution in efforts to eradicate corruption without compromise to ensure that the country’s prosperity can be enjoyed by both present and future generations,” he said via a Facebook post.

items.title: Think leprosy is gone? Kelantan recorded eight new cases last year
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, June 12 — Leprosy still exists in Kelantan with eight new cases recorded last year, despite many believing that the disease had been eradicated and was no longer a public health problem. Kelantan health director Datuk Dr Mohd Azman Yacob said the number involved an incidence rate of 0.37 cases per 100,000 population, while at the national level, 381 new cases were reported in 2025 with an incidence rate of 11.5 cases per 100,000 population. However, this disease can be completely cured if patients receive treatment consistently,” he said in a statement in conjunction with the Kelantan State Level World Leprosy Day 2026 and the Tumpat District Level Health Literacy Meet yesterday.

items.title: TikToker arrested over alleged defamatory claims involving rescued hiker Jaslinda
items.summary: IPOH, June 12 — Police have arrested a man who is believed to have defamed and tarnished the reputation of the Tok Batin (village chief) of Kampung Orang Asli Lubuk Gaharu, Pos Musoh, in relation to the recent rescue of female hiker Jaslinda Saludin, through a livestream on the TikTok platform. Earlier, he said the investigation papers were opened under Section 500 of the Penal Code, Section 233 of the Communications and Multimedia Act 1998 and Section 14 of the Minor Offences Act 1955, adding that 11 police reports have been received in connection with the case. Yesterday, the man issued a public apology via a video recording following controversy over a livestream on his TikTok account, which allegedly defamed the Tok Batin, Mohd Asmmadi Abdullah, and Jaslinda.

items.title: Family of Zulfiqar Zamir seeks murder probe after alleged drunk-driving crash claims his life
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, June 12 — The family of the late Zulfiqar Zamir Amirul Nizam wants the driver who was allegedly driving under the influence of alcohol, causing the victim to be seriously injured and die while being treated in hospital, to be investigated under Section 302 of the Penal Code. The deceased’s sister, Nur Amirah Afra, 25, said when the investigation was initiated, the case was being investigated under Section 44(1A) of the Road Transport Act 1987 because her brother was still alive, but after the victim’s death, the family hopes that the investigation can be conducted under Section 302 of the Penal Code. Nur Amirah Afra said the deceased died 18 days ago after 13 days fighting for his life in hospital, but the family is still waiting for the progress of the investigation from the authorities and is providing full cooperation to the investigating officers.
